summary:
The primary focus should be minimizing risk factors for severe perineal tearing or obstetric anal sphincter injury during vaginal deliveries; however, among common post-delivery issues directly linked to instrument use like forceps, conservative treatment approaches effectively manage vulvar hematoma cases without prolonged morbidity concerns. Outlet Forceps thus have fewer complex complications needing intensive care when contrasted against potential outcomes involving major lacerations or organ damage. To answer your query succinctly,

The least complicated issue arising most frequently due to outlet forcep application involves managing vulva hematomas which typically require minimal interventions leading to quick recovery periods unlike serious tear scenarios often encountered irrespective of surgical incisions made via episiotomy procedures. Thus, considering all aspects discussed here, it remains accurate stating "hematoma" represents one aspect where outcome complexities are lower than others seen clinically related closely tied back under expert supervision ensuring optimal patient safety standards maintained throughout labor progression phases until full term birth completion occurs successfully avoiding unnecessary adverse effects wherever possible while prioritizing overall well-being across multiple dimensions impacting mothers physically emotionally mentally socially financially etcetera accordingly based off current
